defects.symptom: `dummyindex install` (any scope, even `--scope user` run only to resync skill files) destructively rebuilds an existing curated .context/: full AST re-scan + community re-cluster, rewriting features/INDEX.json and ~20 tracked backbone files and creating community-N stub dirs (824 in one session), destroying the council-enriched taxonomy. Covers evidence items 'Bare install executed a full destructive install and re-clustered curated .context' (x2, critical) and the installer side of 'install ran a destructive index rebuild as a side effect'.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. install.py:175-179 unconditionally auto-inits whenever the resolved target (cwd by default) is a git repo, and _auto_init_project (install.py:223) calls build_all() directly. build_all has NO enriched-index guard — it unconditionally re-runs scaffold_features (runner.py:196-204) and writes a fresh new_meta (runner.py:128). The non-destructive guard `_is_enriched_index` exists ONLY inside rebuild_changed (incremental.py:137,167) — a private function the installer never goes through. skills/update/SKILL.md:76 even (falsely) documents install's build as 'non-destructive', so the shipped /dummyindex-update skill actively drives this destructive path.
defects.proposed_fix: In _auto_init_project (install.py), before calling build_all: if (project_root/'.context').is_dir() and the index is enriched, take the non-destructive path instead — call refresh_deterministic_artifacts(project_root, extra_doc_roots=()) (already exported from dummyindex.context.build), still run bootstrap_claude_md + install_hooks_fn, and print 'curated index detected — preserved; refreshed N deterministic artefact(s)'. Promote incremental._is_enriched_index to a public export (e.g. `is_enriched_index` in dummyindex/context/build/__init__.py, keep the underscore alias) rather than duplicating the bias-to-preserve logic. Deterministic-only or absent .context keeps today's build_all path unchanged. Pass dummyindex_version=PACKAGE_VERSION into the refresh (see the meta-stamp defect). Then correct skills/update/SKILL.md:76 so the 'non-destructive' claim is true. Design decision for implementer: whether install should touch .context at all when an index already exists — recommended: refresh-only (matches the /dummyindex-update contract: 'never touching curated taxonomy'); a re-cluster must require explicit `rebuild --full` or a fresh ingest.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/test_install.py: build a tmp git repo (reuse _make_repo_with_source idiom at test_install.py:308), run install() once, then mark the index curated (rename a community-N feature dir + its INDEX.json id to a named feature, or set confidence INFERRED), re-run install(); assert features/INDEX.json byte-identical, no new community-* dirs, hooks still installed, CLAUDE.md managed block intact. Counter-test: a deterministic-only index still full-builds. Unit test for the new public is_enriched_index export.

defects.symptom: Re-running `dummyindex install` rewrote .claude/settings.json and silently DROPPED a user-added Stop hook (DUMMYINDEX_BACKBONE_REFRESH) — 'the canonical install only re-adds memory-nudge + reconcile-gate' (v0.25.0 report). REPRODUCED at HEAD in a throwaway temp repo: a user hook added inside the managed Stop entry's hooks array is deleted by the next hooks install whenever the canonical body differs; a user hook in a separate entry survives.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED by reproduction. install_hook_entry's refresh-in-place (claude_settings.py:84-93) does `events[idx] = hook_body` — it replaces the ENTIRE matched entry ({matcher, hooks:[...]}) with the canonical body when any hook in the entry carries the sentinel. Any non-sentinel hook the user added inside that entry (the natural place for an agent to wire a custom Stop hook, since Claude Code groups hooks within entries) is silently discarded. The canonical Stop body changed across 0.23-0.25 (reconcile-gate added), so every upgrade re-install fires the replacement. This violates the module's own documented contract ('preserving user entries').
defects.proposed_fix: Make the refresh a per-hook merge instead of an entry-wholesale replace. In install_hook_entry, when the sentinel-matched entry is found: preserved = [h for h in entry.get('hooks', []) if sentinel not in (h.get('command') or '')]; desired = {**hook_body, 'hooks': [*hook_body['hooks'], *preserved]} (new dicts, per the immutability convention); if events[idx] == desired return False (no-op), else events[idx] = desired, write_settings, return False. Keep the canonical matcher (design decision: canonical hooks first, preserved user hooks appended after — document the ordering). No change needed in remove_hook_entries (it already filters per-entry by sentinel, dropping co-located user hooks on uninstall too — implementer should apply the same per-hook filtering there: strip sentinel hooks from an entry and KEEP the entry if non-sentinel hooks remain).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/test_claude_settings.py: (1) install canonical entry; append a non-sentinel hook into the managed entry's hooks array; call install_hook_entry again with a CHANGED canonical body; assert the user hook survives AND the canonical hooks are refreshed. (2) idempotence: second identical call writes nothing. (3) uninstall/remove_hook_entries on an entry containing both sentinel and user hooks keeps the user hook. tests/context/test_hooks.py: end-to-end install() upgrade regression mirroring the reproduction script.

defects.symptom: No machine version-coherence check anywhere: the skills' only freshness guard is passive prose ('confirm the CLI matches'), so equally-stale CLI+skill pairs (0.15/0.15) pass forever; 4-layer skew (CLI 0.23 / skills 0.22 / repo dep 0.22 / meta.json 0.15) goes undetected; a repo-venv 0.15 dummyindex silently shadows the upgraded global 0.25 CLI; agents repeatedly noticed divergence and proceeded anyway. Covers ~12 evidence items (0, 4, 9, 10, 13, 14, 16, 18, 24, 27, 28, 29).
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. The `.dummyindex_version` stamp written at install.py:143 has zero readers in the codebase (grep: only writer + uninstall). meta.json's dummyindex_version (meta.py:19) has no consumer that compares it to the running PACKAGE_VERSION. The SessionStart hook commands (hooks.py:63-87: plan-update + memory session-start) surface no version information. No code compares Path(sys.argv[0]) against shutil.which('dummyindex') to detect a venv-shadowed stale binary. The banner at skills/skill.md:8 (and each sibling SKILL.md:9) compares only skill-vs-CLI and is prose the model skims; both-stale is structurally undetectable by it.
defects.proposed_fix: Add a `dummyindex context version-check` verb: thin run() handler in dummyindex/cli/ (folder-restructure convention) + a domain module (e.g. dummyindex/context/versioncheck.py) returning a frozen dataclass with the four layers: (1) running CLI = importlib.metadata version, plus resolved binary path (Path(sys.argv[0]).resolve()) vs shutil.which('dummyindex') to flag PATH shadowing/venv copies; (2) repo skill stamp <root>/.claude/skills/dummyindex/.dummyindex_version, falling back to ~/.claude/...; (3) .context/meta.json dummyindex_version; (4) optional consumer-repo pin (best-effort uv.lock/pyproject scan — design decision whether to include). Print one warning line per mismatch + a nudge to run /dummyindex-update; exit 0 always (warn-only). Wire it as a third command into _SESSION_START_HOOK (context/hooks.py) so skew lands in every session's additionalContext, with the same `command -v` self-gate + defer-check guard pattern. Update the skills banner to add: 'both may be stale together — run /dummyindex-update to check the latest release'. Hard design constraints: NO network at hook time (latest-vs-installed stays in /dummyindex-update); never block.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/cli/test_version_check.py: tmp repo with skill stamp 0.22.0 + meta.json dummyindex_version 0.15.0 vs a monkeypatched running version 0.25.0 → output names both mismatched layers, exit 0; all-matching → single 'coherent' line or silence with --quiet; missing layers (no skill dir, no meta.json) tolerated without error. tests/context/test_hooks.py: installed SessionStart hook body contains the version-check command under the sentinel.

defects.symptom: .context/meta.json's dummyindex_version stamp can ONLY advance via a destructive full rebuild — on a curated index it froze at 0.15.0/0.19.2 across multiple CLI upgrades, fueling skew misdiagnosis ('the stale 0.19.2 index stamp was wrongly blamed for the re-shatter'), and the only sanctioned way to move it is exactly the re-cluster that destroys the taxonomy.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. _refresh_meta (enriched_refresh.py:184-213) re-stamps only updated_at + file_count/symbol_count ('All other meta fields are preserved'); rebuild_changed's dummyindex_version parameter (incremental.py:65) is consumed only by the full build_all branch (incremental.py:158-164), never the preserved-enriched branch (137-155); and stamp_reconciled (reconcile.py:189) writes with_updates(indexed_commit=head) only — Meta.with_updates preserves dummyindex_version. So every non-destructive path leaves the version stamp frozen at whatever destructive build created the index.
defects.proposed_fix: Thread the version through the non-destructive paths. (1) Add `dummyindex_version: str | None = None` to refresh_deterministic_artifacts and _refresh_meta; include it in the with_updates call when provided. (2) rebuild_changed passes its existing dummyindex_version into the enriched branch's refresh call (incremental.py:143). (3) Add optional `dummyindex_version` to stamp_reconciled, passed from cli/reconcile.py run_stamp (resolve importlib.metadata.version at the CLI boundary — the build layer must not import installer constants). (4) The install non-destructive path (P0 defect above) passes PACKAGE_VERSION. Document the field's semantics in meta.py: 'the dummyindex version that last wrote or refreshed this index'. This also unblocks /dummyindex-update's layer-3 verification (see the SKILL.md defect).
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context/build: enriched fixture index → rebuild --changed (or rebuild_changed direct) with dummyindex_version='9.9.9' → meta.dummyindex_version == '9.9.9', features/INDEX.json untouched, indexed_commit NOT advanced (Model B preserved). stamp_reconciled with dummyindex_version set advances both indexed_commit and the version; without it, version is preserved (backward compat).

defects.symptom: `dummyindex install --help` is rejected ('error: unknown install argument \'--help\'', exit 2 — reproduced at HEAD with python3 -m dummyindex), and a bare `dummyindex install` run to probe usage immediately executes a full install including AST-scanning the cwd repo (1426-file monorepo from the wrong root in one session). Probing the command IS running it.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. parse_install_args (args.py:19-52) has no '-h'/'--help' branch — they fall into the unknown-argument error. Top-level help exists only for `dummyindex --help` (__main__.py:206). Bare `install` then proceeds: skill copy + auto-init of cwd-as-project (install.py:175-179) with no usage text, confirmation, or target announcement, defaulting the index root to cwd (the superproject in a monorepo).
defects.proposed_fix: Handle '-h'/'--help' first in parse_install_args: print the install/uninstall section of the usage text (extract a small _print_install_usage() shared with __main__._print_help to avoid drift) and sys.exit(0). Apply the same to the uninstall arg path. Additionally print the resolved auto-init target ('project init target: <abs path> — pass --skill-only to skip') BEFORE starting the AST scan so a wrong-root run is visible immediately. Design decision: whether to require --yes / interactive confirmation when auto-init would create a brand-new .context in a repo above a size threshold — recommend the announcement line as the minimal fix, with the P0 enriched-guard making existing indexes safe regardless.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/test_install.py: parse_install_args(['--help']) raises SystemExit(0) and prints usage (capsys), no side effects; parse_install_args(['-h']) same; existing unknown-flag test still passes; install() output contains the resolved init target line.

defects.symptom: Installer ships 9 inert equip render-template copies (*.md.tmpl) into <scope>/.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates/ that nothing ever reads; deleting them changes nothing (equip renders fine), the next install silently re-copies them, and they pollute reconcile's unassigned-files list in consumer repos.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. install.py:132-140 copies BOTH 'templates' and 'agents' companion dirs for every sibling skill. But the equip renderer resolves templates strictly package-relative: render.py:38 `_TEMPLATES_DIR = Path(_skills_pkg.__file__).resolve().parent / 'equip' / 'templates'` — the site-packages copy, never the .claude copy — and equip's SKILL.md contains zero references to a templates/ path. By contrast audit's 'agents/' companion IS consumed (skills/audit/SKILL.md:49 instructs the orchestrator to Read agents/<persona_id>.md beside the installed SKILL.md), so the generic copy loop is half right: agents must ship, templates must not.
defects.proposed_fix: Replace the generic companion tuple ('templates', 'agents') with a per-skill companion map in install.py, e.g. _SIBLING_SKILLS: tuple[tuple[str, str, tuple[str, ...]], ...] = (('plan','dummyindex-plan',()), ('equip','dummyindex-equip',()), ('build','dummyindex-build',()), ('audit','dummyindex-audit',('agents',)), ('update','dummyindex-update',())) — module-level constant per conventions. Also purge a previously-installed dummyindex-equip/templates/ dir on upgrade (mirror the stale-companion wipe pattern at install.py:89-90) so existing installs get cleaned. Update the install.py:127-131 comment, which currently asserts the renderer needs the copies ('equip's render templates/') — that belief is what shipped the bug.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/test_install.py: after install(), assert (base/'.claude/skills/dummyindex-equip/templates').exists() is False while (base/'.claude/skills/dummyindex-audit/agents') contains the persona *.md files; upgrade test: pre-seed a stale templates/ dir with a .tmpl file, re-run install(), assert it was removed. Existing equip render tests already prove package-relative resolution keeps working.

defects.symptom: /dummyindex-update's layer-3 ('per-repo wiring') verification reads a file that does not exist — `.context/cache/_meta.json` — so the `test -f ... && grep` silently no-ops and the skill's promise to 'verify the version actually moved on every layer' can never run for the repo layer; the table at the top documents the same wrong path.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. Grep over the whole package: `_meta.json` appears ONLY in update/SKILL.md (lines 17 and 86). The real stamp is `.context/meta.json` written by write_meta (meta.py:91). `.context/cache/` is gitignored scratch (runner.py:297) and never contains a meta file. Additionally the `test -f X && grep X` construct exits silently when the file is absent, so the broken check produced no error in any session — false 'all layers verified' confidence. (Note: even with the right path, layer 3 cannot move non-destructively until the meta-stamp defect is fixed — the two are coupled.)
defects.proposed_fix: In skills/update/SKILL.md: change both references to `.context/meta.json` (line 17 table cell and the line-86 verification command, e.g. `test -f .context/meta.json && grep -o '"dummyindex_version": *"[^"]*"' .context/meta.json`). Make the step instruct loud failure when meta.json exists but the version did not reach the target. Sequence after the meta-stamp fix lands so the instruction is satisfiable; until then add a parenthetical that a curated index's stamp moves on the next deterministic refresh, not on a re-ingest.
defects.tests_to_add: Lightweight docs-consistency test (tests/test_install.py style, reading the shipped skill source): assert no file under dummyindex/skills/ references '.context/cache/_meta.json', and update/SKILL.md references '.context/meta.json'.

defects.symptom: User-scope CLAUDE.md registration misfires on foreign .context/ trees: at BOS-Mono root (a pre-existing curated docs .context/ with no HOW_TO_USE.md/meta.json) every session is directed to read four nonexistent files; AND the registration block is append-once — any 'dummyindex' string in ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md suppresses the write forever, so shipped fixes to the directive never propagate to existing installs.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. _SKILL_REGISTRATION (common.py:37-41) keys the directive on the bare existence of 'a directory that has a `.context/` folder' with no ownership marker, and prescribes consulting HOW_TO_USE.md etc. unconditionally. install.py:157 (`if "dummyindex" in content`) skips the write whenever the word appears anywhere, so the registration text is frozen at whatever version first wrote it (the user's live ~/.claude/CLAUDE.md is a hand-evolved variant that can never be machine-refreshed).
defects.proposed_fix: (a) Reword _SKILL_REGISTRATION to scope on an ownership marker: 'When working in a repo whose `.context/` contains `HOW_TO_USE.md` (a dummyindex-generated index), read it first…' — one-line change. (b) Convert the user-scope registration to a marker-delimited managed block: reuse the bootstrap_claude_md machinery (context/output/bootstrap.py) with a distinct marker pair (e.g. `<!-- dummyindex:skill-registration:begin/end -->`) and block_body=_SKILL_REGISTRATION, replacing install.py:153-167's substring check — re-install then refreshes the directive in place while preserving surrounding content. Design decision: legacy marker-less registrations ('dummyindex' present, no markers) — recommend leaving the legacy text untouched and appending the marked block is WRONG (duplication); instead print a one-line notice telling the user the registration is hand-managed and how to adopt markers.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/test_install.py: fresh HOME → install creates CLAUDE.md with marker block; second install with a modified shipped body → block refreshed in place, surrounding user content preserved; HOME with legacy marker-less 'dummyindex' mention → no duplicate block, notice printed.

defects.symptom: On a case-sensitive filesystem, a repo whose per-project memory file is `.claude/claude.md` (lowercase, as at BOS-Mono root) gets a SECOND parallel `.claude/CLAUDE.md` created by install/ingest instead of the managed block being appended to the existing file.
defects.root_cause: CONFIRMED. runner.py:257 hard-codes `bootstrap_claude_md(out_root / ".claude" / "CLAUDE.md")`; bootstrap_claude_md (bootstrap.py:43-48) creates the file whenever `path.exists()` is False. There is no case-insensitive lookup for an existing claude.md variant anywhere in the writer path.
defects.proposed_fix: Add a small resolver in output/bootstrap.py: `def resolve_claude_md(claude_dir: Path) -> Path` that scans claude_dir for any existing file whose name lower-cases to 'claude.md' and returns it, else the canonical CLAUDE.md path. Call it from runner.py:257 (and any other bootstrap_claude_md call site — grep shows cli/init + installer reach it via build_all only). Pure function, trivially testable.
defects.tests_to_add: tests/context (alongside existing bootstrap tests): tmp dir with .claude/claude.md containing user prose → build/bootstrap appends the managed block to that file and does NOT create CLAUDE.md; absent → CLAUDE.md created as today; both present (pathological) → prefer exact-case CLAUDE.md.

quick_wins: installer/args.py: accept '-h'/'--help' in parse_install_args (print usage, exit 0) — ~8 lines; kills the 'probing install runs install' trap at its entry point.
skills/update/SKILL.md lines 17 + 86: fix '.context/cache/_meta.json' → '.context/meta.json' so the update skill's layer-3 verification can actually run — 2 lines.
installer/common.py:_SKILL_REGISTRATION: add the ownership qualifier 'a `.context/` folder that contains `HOW_TO_USE.md`' so the global directive stops misfiring on foreign .context/ trees — 1 line.

installer/install.py (_auto_init_project tail): when auto-init ran without --defaults and <root>/.context/config.json is absent, print 'onboarding pending — the first /dummyindex run will ask preferences (or re-run install with --defaults)' — ~4 lines; surfaces the half-initialized state.

Repo hygiene: delete stale gitignored packaging debris at the repo root — dummyindex.egg-info/ (PKG-INFO says Version 0.21.0 vs pyproject 0.25.0) and build/ — avoiding the setuptools stale-build/lib re-bundling trap; optionally add 'rm -rf build *.egg-info' to scripts/release.py.

enriched_refresh.py _refresh_meta: minimal core of the meta-stamp defect — add optional dummyindex_version param and include it in with_updates (~6 lines, plus the one-line pass-through at incremental.py:143).
not_confirmed: 'dummyindex pinned as a project dev dependency ... likely done at equip/install time' — MISDIAGNOSED: no code in the package writes dummyindex into a consumer pyproject/uv.lock (grepped equip domain and whole package); the BOS-Mono dev-dep pin was user-authored. The shadowing it caused is real but environmental; the version-check defect (P1) adds the warning.

'The index stamp only reached 0.25.0 as an incidental side effect of reconcile-stamp' — MISATTRIBUTED at HEAD: stamp_reconciled (context/build/reconcile.py:189) writes only indexed_commit; Meta.with_updates preserves dummyindex_version, so reconcile-stamp cannot move the version stamp. The stamp most likely moved via a full rebuild during incident recovery.

CLI vanished from ~/.local/bin mid-session / uv cache debris (14 leftover editable-install hash dirs) / snap-revision off-PATH uv-tool install / PEP 668 --break-system-packages removal of the 0.7.0 orphan / duplicate parallel 0.22.0 channels / stale 'dummyindexy' uv receipt — machine/install-channel states with no in-repo code root cause at HEAD; mitigation is the version-check doctor's channel/shadowing report plus /dummyindex-update docs (which already detect uv tool/pipx/pip --user and explicitly defer to 'mirror where which dummyindex resolves' for venv installs).

'Installed CLI payload one commit behind repo HEAD while version strings match' — inherent to the release cadence (release.yml bumps only on push; local feat commits between releases are invisible to version comparison); not a code defect; /dummyindex-update reinstalls from the release tag, which is self-consistent.

'Onboarding cost copy assumes ~14 features rather than reading the actual index (734 communities)' — lives in the onboarding skill prose, another cluster's territory (onboarding/council); only the installer-side 'half-built state unannounced' part is actionable here (quick win 4).

'Pre-commit blanket exclusion of .context/ + vendored skill' and 'vendored skill committed into the consumer repo drifts/collides with CI' — consumer-repo workarounds; no dummyindex-owned code path writes or owns consumer lint config; at most a docs enhancement (ship recommended pre-commit excludes in README/skill docs). Not a confirmable code defect in this repo.
